title: Seborrheic dermatitis
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports seborrheic-dermatitis and dandruff terminology, scalp or facial scale, pruritus, greasy plaques, erythema, scalp, eyebrow, nasolabial, eyelid, ear, trunk, fold, relapsing, stress, and seasonal context.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Seborrheic Dermatitis
source.population: Infants, adolescents, and adults with suspected or clinician-diagnosed seborrheic dermatitis, with differential and referral qualifiers.
source.setting: DHA-licensed telehealth assessment with face-to-face or dermatology referral where indicated.
source.exclusions: Direct for selected seborrheic-dermatitis symptoms, distribution, appearance, red flags, differential testing, and documentation-only management fields. Diagnosis, medicine choice, dose, treatment instruction, referral urgency, and fixed follow-up remain clinician responsibilities.

assessment.text: Supports fever or systemic illness, painful rapidly worsening eczema, recurrent or spreading infection, and clinician-documented worsening or return fields.
assessment.rationale: Supports fever or systemic illness, painful worsening lesions, spreading infection, and clinician-documented worsening or return fields.

exact_location.heading: Section 6 — red flags

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports fever or systemic illness, painful rapidly worsening eczema, recurrent or spreading infection, and clinician-documented worsening or return fields.

assessment.text: Supports clinician-reviewed family history in psoriasis context and KOH or fungal-culture documentation when tinea corporis is a differential, without inferring either diagnosis.
assessment.rationale: Supports clinician-reviewed family history and scalp or prior-treatment context without inferring a differential.
